prompt: Our search gateway is slowly eating itself. Functionally it looks fine — fanout.Search returns the first replica hit fast, aggregation over total failures works — but after a day in production the goroutine count is in the tens of thousands and a pprof goroutine dump is wall-to-wall `fanout.Search.func1` parked in `chan send`, plus a pile of replicas parked in `select` waiting on a context that never gets cancelled. It's worst on requests where the caller gave up and cancelled, and on requests where one replica answered instantly while the slow ones were still working. We added the wait() handle precisely so request scopes could confirm their backends were done, and it just never returns in those cases. Fix fanout.go so wait() always comes back once backends honor cancellation — without breaking first-hit semantics, the joined all-failure error, or Collect's closed-channel contract. fanout_test.go stays as is. Verify: go test -race -timeout 30s ./...

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0. On the baseline, TestEarlySuccessReleasesBackends and TestCallerCancellationReleasesBackends fail after their 2s bounded waits: wait() never returns because loser goroutines stay blocked sending on the unbuffered results channel and siblings are never cancelled after early return.
